passages.title: Ambergris
passages.source_html:
Ambergris 


What is it and where does it come from? 


A 


Ambergris was used to perfume cosmetics in the days of ancient Mesopotamia and almost every civilization on the earth has a brush with ambergris. Before 1,000 AD, the Chinese names ambergris as lung sien hiang, ‘dragon’s spittle perfume’, as they think that it was produced from the drooling of dragons sleeping on rocks at the edge of a sea. The Arabs knew ambergris as anbar, believing that it is produced from springs near seas. It also gets its name from here. For centuries, this substance has also been used as a flavouring for food. 


B 


During the Middle Ages, Europeans used ambergris as a remedy for headaches,colds, epilepsy, and other ailments. In the 1851 whaling novel Moby-Dick, Herman Melville claimed that ambergris was ‘largely used in perfumery’. But nobody ever knew where it really came from. Experts were still guessing its origin thousands of years later, until the long ages of guesswork ended in the 1720’s, when Nantucket whalers found gobs of the costly material inside the stomachs of sperm whales. Industrial whaling quickly burgeoned. By 20th century ambergris is mainly recovered from inside the carcasses of sperm whales. 


C 


Through countless ages, people have found pieces of ambergris on sandy beaches. It was named grey amber to distinguish it from golden amber, another rare treasure. Both of them were among the most sought-after substances in the world, almost as valuable as gold. Ambergris sells for roughly $20 a gram, slightly less than gold at $30 a gram. Amber floats in salt water, and in old times the origin of both these substances was mysterious. But it turned out that amber and ambergris have little in common. Amber is a fossilized resin from trees that was quite familiar to Europeans long before the discovery of the New World, and prized as jewelry. Although considered a gem, amber is a hard,transparent, wholly-organic material derived from the resin of extinct species of trees, mainly pines. 


D 


To the earliest Western chroniclers, ambergris was variously thought to come from the same bituminous sea founts as amber,from the sperm of fishes or whales, from the droppings of strange sea birds (probably because of confusion over the included beaks of squid) or from the large hives of bees living near the sea. Marco Polo was the first Western chronicler who correctly attributed ambergris to sperm whales and its vomit. 


E 


As sperm whales navigate in the oceans, they often dive down to 2 km or more below the sea level to prey on squid, most famously the Giant Squid. It’s commonly accepted that ambergris forms in the whale’s gut or intestines as the creature attempts to ‘deal’ with squid beaks. Sperm whales are rather partial to squid, but seemingly struggle to digest the hard, sharp, parrot-like beaks. It is thought their stomach juices become hyper-active trying to process the irritants, and eventually hard, resinous lumps are formed around the beaks, and then expelled from their innards by vomiting. When a whale initially vomits up ambergris, it is soft and has a terrible smell. Some marine biologists compare it to the unpleasant smell of cow dung. But after floating on the salty ocean for about a decade, the substance hardens with air and sun into a smooth, waxy, usually rounded piece of nostril heaven. The dung smell is gone, replaced by a sweet, smooth, musky and pleasant earthy aroma. 


F 


Since ambergris is derived from animals, naturally a question of ethics arises, and in the case of ambergris, it is very important to consider. Sperm whales are an endangered species, whose populations started to decline as far back as the 19thcentury due to the high demand for their highly emollient oil, and today their stocks still have not recovered. During the 1970’s, the Save the Whales movement brought the plight of whales to international recognition. Many people now believe that whales are ‘saved’. This couldn’t be further from the truth. All around the world, whaling still exists. Many countries continue to hunt whales, in spite of international treaties to protect them. Many marine researchers are concerned that even the trade in naturally found ambergris can be harmful by creating further incentives to hunt whales for this valuable substance.


G 


One of the forms ambergris is used today is as a valuable fixative in perfumes to enhance and prolong the scent. But nowadays,since ambergris is rare and expensive, and big fragrance suppliers that make most of the fragrances on the market today do not deal in it for reasons of cost, availability and murky legal issues,most perfumeries prefer to add a chemical derivative which mimics the properties of ambergris. As a fragrance consumer, you can assume that there is no natural ambergris in your perfume bottle, unless the company advertises this fact and unless you own vintage fragrances created before the 1980s. If you are wondering if you have been wearing a perfume with this legendary ingredient, you may want to review your scent collection. Here are a few of some of the top ambergris containing perfumes: Givenchy Amarige, Chanel No. 5, and Gucci Guilty.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1-6


Classify the following information as referring to Write the correct letter A, B, C, or D in boxes 1-6 on your answer sheet.


A ambergris only 

B amber only 

C both ambergris and amber 

D neither ambergris nor amber 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
1

1 being expensive
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Both of them were among the most sought-after substances in the world, almost as valuable as gold. Ambergris sells for roughly $20 a gram, slightly less than gold at $30 a gram. 

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是哪种物质十分昂贵。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“expensive”定位到C段，该段提到了ambergris和amber的珍贵程度。  

解题思路：原文明确提到“Both of them were among the most sought-after substances in the world, almost as valuable as gold”，说明龙涎香和琥珀都因其稀有而昂贵。随后还具体比较了龙涎香和黄金的价格。因此答案为C。
2

2 adds flavor to food
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：For centuries, this substance has also been used as a flavouring for food.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是哪个物质“用于给食物调味”。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"adds flavor to food"定位到A段中的"used as a flavouring for food"。

解题思路：A段提到了龙涎香在古代被用作化妆品的香料，并且几乎每个文明都与龙涎香有过接触。在A段的后半部分，明确提到了龙涎香被用作食物的调味料已有几个世纪的历史。因此，A段是正确答案。
3

3 used as currency
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Ambergris was used to perfume cosmetics in the days of ancient Mesopotamia and almost every civilization on the earth has a brush with ambergris...For centuries, this substance has also been used as a flavouring for food. 

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是哪种物质“作为货币使用”。  

定位关键信息：题目中的“used as currency”是关键词，需要找到龙涎香和琥珀香的用途有关的信息。  

解题思路：A段提到了龙涎香在古代被用作化妆品的香料，并且几乎每个文明都与龙涎香有过接触。在A段的后半部分，明确提到了龙涎香被用作食物的调味料已有几个世纪的历史，但未提及货币用途。后续也并未提到琥珀作为货币使用过。因此，选择D选项。
4

4 being see-through
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Although considered a gem, amber is a hard, transparent, wholly-organic material derived from the resin of extinct species of trees, mainly pines.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目询问哪个物质是“透明”的。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的“being see-through”这一描述，在文章中寻找与“透明”相关的信息。在C段中找到“transparent”这一关键词，明确指向琥珀的特性。

解题思路：原文中明确提到琥珀是透明的（transparent），而题干中的“being see-through”正是对“transparent”的同义转述。因此，正确答案为B，即B段中描述的琥珀具有透明这一特性。
5

5 referred to by Herman Melville
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：In the 1851 whaling novel Moby-Dick, Herman Melville claimed that ambergris was ‘largely used in perfumery’.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是Herman Melville提到了哪种物质。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词Herman Melville，在文章中定位到B段，找到包含该名字的句子。

解题思路：根据原文答案句中的信息，可以确定Herman Melville在《白鲸》一书中提到了龙涎香。因此，答案为A。
6

6 produces sweet smell
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：When a whale initially vomits up ambergris, it is soft and has a terrible smell. But after floating on the salty ocean for about a decade, the substance hardens with air and sun into a smooth, waxy, usually rounded piece of nostril heaven. The dung smell is gone, replaced by a sweet, smooth, musky and pleasant earthy aroma.  

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目问的是哪种物质“散发甜香”。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“produces sweet smell”定位到E段中描述龙涎香气味变化的句子。  

解题思路：E段详细描述了龙涎香从最初呕吐时的恶臭气味，经过海水漂浮和日晒后，气味逐渐转变为“sweet, smooth, musky and pleasant earthy aroma”，即甜香。因此，选择A。

Questions 7-9


Complete the sentences below with NO MORE THAN ONE WORDS from the passage. Write your answers in boxes 7-9 on your answer sheet.


7

7 Sperm whales can’t digest the of the squids.

正确答案： 7 beaks##Beaks
Question 7

原文答案句：Sperm whales are rather partial to squid, but seemingly struggle to digest the hard, sharp, parrot-like beaks.  

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目问的是抹香鲸无法消化鱿鱼哪个部位。  

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"squids"定位到E段，该段详细描述了抹香鲸消化鱿鱼的困难所在。  

解题思路：根据原文答案句可知，抹香鲸对鱿鱼情有独钟，但似乎难以消化那些坚硬锋利、形似鹦鹉的喙部。因此答案为beaks。


8

8 Sperm whales drive the irritants out of their intestines by .

正确答案： 8 vomiting##Vomiting
Question 8

原文答案句：It is thought their stomach juices become hyper-active trying to process the irritants, and eventually hard, resinous lumps are formed around the beaks, and then expelled from their innards by vomiting.  

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目问的是抹香鲸如何将刺激物排出肠道。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的"Sperm whales"和"irritants"定位到E段，该段描述了抹香鲸处理鱿鱼喙的过程。  

解题思路：原文提到抹香鲸的胃液会变得异常活跃以处理刺激物（squid beaks），最终形成硬块并从体内排出，而排出的方式是通过"vomiting"（呕吐）。因此，抹香鲸是通过呕吐将刺激物排出肠道的。


9

9 The vomit of sperm whale gradually on contact of air before having pleasant smell.

正确答案： 9 hardens##Hardens
Question 9

原文答案句：When a whale initially vomits up ambergris, it is soft and has a terrible smell. But after floating on the salty ocean for about a decade, the substance hardens with air and sun into a smooth, waxy, usually rounded piece of nostril heaven. 

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目问的是抹香鲸的呕吐物在接触空气后逐渐如何变化，最终产生令人愉悦的气味。  

定位关键信息：根据题干中的关键词"vomit of sperm whale"（抹香鲸的呕吐物）和"pleasant smell"（令人愉悦的气味），可以定位到E段描述抹香鲸呕吐物变化过程的句子。  

解题思路：原文E段明确提到抹香鲸的呕吐物最初是柔软且难闻，但经过与空气和阳光接触后，物质会硬化，最终形成光滑、蜡质、通常呈圆形的物质，产生好闻的气味。题目描述的"逐渐在接触空气后硬化"与原文中的"hardens with air"完全一致，因此答案为"hardens"。


Questions 10-13


Do the following statements agree with the information given in the passage? In boxes 10-13 on your answer sheet, write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.


10

Most ambergris comes from the dead whales today.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：By 20th century ambergris is mainly recovered from inside the carcasses of sperm whales.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是“如今大多数龙涎香来自死鲸”，需要判断这个说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“Most ambergris”和“dead whales today”来定位答案。在B段中找到“By 20th century ambergris is mainly recovered from inside the carcasses of sperm whales.”这句话，其中的“carcasses”意为“尸体”，与“dead whales”对应，“mainly recovered”意为“主要回收”，与“Most ambergris”对应。

解题思路：原文明确提到“20世纪龙涎香主要从抹香鲸的尸体中回收”，这与题目中的“如今大多数龙涎香来自死鲸”一致，因此可以判断题目说法与文章信息一致，答案为True。


11

Ambergris is becoming more expensive than before.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Both of them were among the most sought-after substances in the world, almost as valuable as gold. Ambergris sells for roughly $20 a gram, slightly less than gold at $30 a gram.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目判断“龙涎香变得比以前更贵了”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“more expensive than before”（比以前更贵）定位信息。

解题思路：段落C中，原文给出了龙涎香当前的价格，并说明它和琥珀都是历史上“最受追捧的物质之一，价值几乎与黄金相当”。然而，原文只提供了某个时间点的价格和历史上的价值地位，并未将现在的价格与过去某个特定时期的价格进行对比。因此，题目中的比较关系在原文中未提及，无法确定龙涎香是否比以前更贵。故答案为Not Given。


12

Ambergris is still the most frequently used ingredient in perfume production today.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Nowadays, since ambergris is rare and expensive, and big fragrance suppliers that make most of the fragrances on the market today do not deal in it for reasons of cost, availability and murky legal issues, most perfumeries prefer to add a chemical derivative which mimics the properties of ambergris.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问“龙涎香是否仍然是当今香水生产中最常用的成分”。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"Ambergris"、"most frequently used ingredient in perfume production today"可以定位到G段中关于龙涎香在当今香水行业使用情况的描述。

解题思路：根据G段原文，龙涎香由于稀有、昂贵以及法律问题，大型的香水供应商现在不再使用它，而是更倾向于使用化学衍生物来模拟其特性。原文明确指出“most perfumeries prefer to add a chemical derivative which mimics the properties of ambergris”，这意味着龙涎香不再是香水生产中最常用的成分。因此，题目的说法与原文信息矛盾，答案为False。


13

New uses of ambergris have been discovered recently.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：One of the forms ambergris is used today is as a valuable fixative in perfumes to enhance and prolong the scent. 

对应段落：G段

的最审题：题目判断“近期发现了龙涎香的新用途”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"New uses of ambergris"和"discovered recently"在文章中定位，可以找到G段提到了龙涎香的用途。

解题思路：原文详细列举了龙涎香在历史上的多种用途，在古老的美索不达米亚时期用于化妆品香氛，几个世纪以来用作食物调味料，在中世纪用作药物，G段提及了在现代主要用作香水定香剂。但文中没有任何信息表明最近发现了任何新的、以前未知的用途。因此，答案是Not Given。
